name: Binomial tree (CRR) European call
task: Create /tmp/eval_crr/crr.py with binomial_call(S, K, T, r, sigma, N) returning the price of a European call via a Cox-Ross-Rubinstein binomial tree with N steps. Use dt = T/N, u = exp(sigma*sqrt(dt)), d = 1/u, risk-neutral p = (exp(r*dt) - d) / (u - d), discount exp(-r*dt) per step. Compute terminal payoffs max(S*u^(N-i)*d^i - K, 0) then roll back through the tree.

validation.script: import importlib.util,math; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_crr/crr.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); C = mod.binomial_call; bs = 10.4505835721856; bn = C(100,100,1.0,0.05,0.2,200); assert abs(bn-bs) < 0.1, f'CRR vs BS got {bn}'; d = C(150,100,1.0,0.05,0.2,100); assert abs(d - (150-100*math.exp(-0.05))) < 1.0; assert C(100,1000,1.0,0.05,0.2,100) < 0.01; print('OK')
